data.title: An account with over $11.6 million in profits purchased $200,000 worth of NBA Western Conference First Round Game 5, where the Nuggets defeated the Timberwolves with a point spread.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in Polymarket's prediction event for "NBA Western Conference First Round Game 5 Nuggets vs. Timberwolves," an account with profits exceeding $11.6 million (0x6a72f61820b26b1fe4d956e17b6dc2a1ea3033ee) purchased approximately $200,000 worth of shares in favor of the Nuggets (11.5 points) to defeat the Timberwolves. The average opening price was 50¢, and the current price is 50.5¢, resulting in a floating profit of approximately $1,900. The Nuggets currently lead the NBA Western Conference First Round series 3-1 against the Timberwolves, with Game 5 scheduled for 10:30 AM Beijing time today. Timberwolves star player Anthony Edwards has been diagnosed with a left knee hyperextension and bone contusion, and is currently under weekly observation. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, anticipating changes before pricing.

data.title: An account that had lost over $3 million purchased $270,000 worth of NBA Eastern Conference First Round Game 4, where the Pistons defeated the Magic with the point spread.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in the Polymarket prediction event for "NBA Eastern Conference First Round Game 4 Pistons vs. Magic," an account (0x492442eab586f242b53bda933fd5de859c8a3782) that lost over $3 million purchased approximately $270,000 worth of bets on the Pistons to win by a 3.5-point spread against the Magic. The average opening price was 49.5¢, and the current price is 50.5¢, resulting in a floating profit of approximately $3,800. The NBA Eastern Conference First Round playoff series between the Pistons and Magic is currently 2-1, with the Magic leading. Game 4 will be played at 8:00 AM Beijing time today. The Pistons' starting lineup is O'Sullivan, Harris, Duren, Robinson, and Cunningham; the Magic's starting lineup is Wagner, Bancroft, Carter, Bane, and Sagan. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before prices are priced in.

data.title: Ether Declines 3.4% Amid Repeated Resistance at $2,400
data.description: Ether experienced a 3.4% drop to $2,287 on Monday, marking its fourth unsuccessful attempt to surpass the $2,400 level since April 14. According to NS3.AI, CoinGlass data indicates that there is over $2.5 billion at risk of long liquidation if the price falls below the $2,150 support zone.

data.title: White House Advisor Hints at Upcoming Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Announcement
data.description: A White House cryptocurrency advisor has suggested that a significant announcement regarding the Trump administration's proposed Strategic Bitcoin Reserve might be made in the coming weeks. According to NS3.AI, this development could have substantial implications for the cryptocurrency market and U.S. economic strategy. The advisor's comments have sparked interest and speculation among investors and analysts, who are eager to understand the potential impact of such a reserve on Bitcoin's market dynamics and the broader financial landscape. The proposed reserve is expected to play a crucial role in shaping the administration's approach to digital currencies and their integration into national economic policies.

data.title: Japan's March Job-to-Applicant Ratio Matches Expectations at 1.18
data.description: Japan's job-to-applicant ratio for March was reported at 1.18, aligning with market expectations, according to Jin10. This figure represents a slight decrease from the previous month's ratio of 1.19, indicating a minor shift in the job market dynamics.

data.title: Israel Approves Shekel-Pegged Stablecoin Amid Crypto Regulation Efforts
data.description:
Israel's Capital Market, Insurance and Savings Authority has approved the launch of a shekel-pegged stablecoin by the virtual exchange Bits of Gold. According to Cointelegraph, the Israeli regulator announced on Monday that it had granted approval for the BILS stablecoin following a two-year pilot program conducted on the Solana blockchain. The stablecoin's reserve assets will be maintained in Israel within designated and separate accounts. This initiative is part of a broader effort by the Israel Tax Authority and the country's Finance Ministry to regulate the cryptocurrency industry, including permitting certain stablecoin activities.

Bits of Gold founder and CEO Youval Rouach stated that BILS establishes a direct connection between the Israeli shekel and the global digital assets economy, facilitating real-time payments, on-chain trading, and programmable financial applications based on a regulated local currency. As of Monday, the global stablecoin market capitalization exceeded $320 billion, with US dollar-pegged stablecoins like Tether's USDt (USDT) dominating the market. The introduction of BILS, one of the first Israeli shekel-pegged coins, coincides with the fiat currency reaching a 30-year high against the US dollar, valued at 1 ILS to 0.34 USD at the time of publication.

Meanwhile, in the United States, lawmakers are engaged in ongoing discussions regarding a digital asset market structure bill, focusing on stablecoin yield, tokenized equities, and ethical concerns related to U.S. President Donald Trump's potential conflicts of interest with the industry. The legislation has been stalled in the US Senate since July 2025, pending a markup by the chamber's banking committee before it can proceed to a potential vote.

data.title: Japan's March Unemployment Rate Announcement Imminent
data.description: Japan is set to release its March unemployment rate in ten minutes. According to Jin10, this data will provide insights into the country's labor market conditions for the month.

data.title: UK Retailers Offer Discounts Amid Geopolitical Tensions, BRC Reports
data.description: On April 28, geopolitical tensions have intensified, prompting UK retailers to offer discounts to stimulate consumer spending, according to Jin10. This strategy has contributed to a cooling of store price inflation in April. The British Retail Consortium (BRC) reported that the annual rate of the BRC shop price index decreased from 1.2% in March to 1% in April. Food inflation fell from 3.4% to 3.1% over the same period, while non-food item prices saw a year-on-year decline of 0.1%, reversing the 0.1% inflation increase observed in March. Helen Dickinson, CEO of the BRC, noted that retailers have intensified price competition to encourage more spring purchases amid weakening consumer confidence. She mentioned, "While we have not yet seen the full impact of Middle Eastern conflicts on consumer prices, this effect is expected to become apparent soon."

data.title: Asian Markets Expected to Open Weak Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
data.description: Asian stock markets are anticipated to open weak on Tuesday, April 28, as geopolitical uncertainties persist. According to Jin10, investors are shifting their focus to the earnings reports of major U.S. tech giants, which collectively have a market capitalization of nearly $16 trillion (16万亿美元). Walter Todd, President and Chief Investment Officer of Greenwood Capital Associates, noted that the U.S. stock market has rebounded to historical highs, seemingly disregarding ongoing geopolitical risks. This resilience is largely attributed to upward revisions in earnings expectations and improved market sentiment. However, Todd cautioned that any cracks in this optimistic outlook, as companies release their earnings in the coming weeks, could pose significant risks to market trends, potentially hindering the rally of large tech stocks.

data.title: Colorado and xAI Seek Court Delay on AI Law Challenge
data.description: Colorado and xAI have jointly requested a federal court to pause deadlines in xAI's legal challenge against SB24-205. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as lawmakers are considering amendments to the state's AI legislation. Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser announced that the state will refrain from enforcing SB24-205 or any new law from this session until the legislative and rulemaking processes are finalized.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Decrease
data.description: The world's largest gold ETF, SPDR Gold Trust, reported a reduction in its holdings by 2.285 tons, bringing the current total to 1,044.337 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects recent market activities and investor sentiment towards gold.

data.title: CME Reports 100% Probability of Fed Holding Rates Steady in April
data.description: On April 28, Jin10 reported that according to CME's 'FedWatch', there is a 100% probability that the U.S. Federal Reserve will maintain its interest rates unchanged in April. Furthermore, the probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting rates by 25 basis points by June stands at 4.5%, while the likelihood of keeping rates steady is at 95.5%.

data.title: ZetaChain Halts Cross-Chain Transactions Following GatewayEVM Contract Attack
data.description: ZetaChain has temporarily suspended cross-chain transactions after an attack on its GatewayEVM contract. According to NS3.AI, the company reported that the incident was limited to internal team wallets and did not compromise user funds. ZetaChain assured that the attack vector has been contained, and an investigation is ongoing with a post-mortem analysis planned.

data.title: Bitcoin 2026 Conference Faces Criticism for Straying from Anti-Establishment Roots
data.description: Bitcoin 2026 kicked off on Monday in Las Vegas, drawing criticism from early adopters who argue that the event has deviated from Bitcoin's original anti-establishment ethos. According to NS3.AI, the conference, organized by BTC Inc., is anticipated to attract over 40,000 attendees and feature 500 speakers by Wednesday at The Venetian.

data.title: DOJ's New Stance on Software Developers in Criminal Cases
data.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced that software developers who do not assist third parties in committing crimes will not face investigation or charges from the Department of Justice (DOJ). According to NS3.AI, Blanche emphasized that the department has significantly altered its approach towards developers, even as it proceeds with ongoing cases involving Tornado Cash and Samourai Wallet.

data.title: Avi Eisenberg Denies Threatening Aave Following Wallet Signature Link
data.description: Avi Eisenberg has refuted claims that he threatened to attack Aave after Arkham Intelligence connected a new wallet signature to him. Eisenberg, who was previously convicted for the $110 million exploit of Mango Markets, stated that he had privately informed the Aave team of a potential risk in 2022, rather than targeting the protocol. According to NS3.AI, Eisenberg emphasized that his actions were aimed at alerting the team to vulnerabilities rather than posing a direct threat.

data.title: Tennessee to Ban Cryptocurrency Kiosks Starting July 1
data.description: Tennessee will prohibit the use and installation of cryptocurrency kiosks beginning July 1. According to NS3.AI, Governor Bill Lee has signed legislation that categorizes the installation of these kiosks as a Class A misdemeanor. Businesses and operators involved in setting up the kiosks could face penalties of up to 11 months and 29 days in prison, along with a $2,500 fine.

data.title: Wind Developers Shift Focus to Fossil Fuels Under Trump Administration Agreements
data.description: Two offshore wind developers have decided to abandon their federal leases in favor of investing in fossil fuel projects, according to the Interior Department's announcement on Monday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in strategy as part of agreements with the Trump administration. This move marks a significant change in the energy sector, as developers redirect their focus from renewable energy to traditional fossil fuel investments. The decision comes amid ongoing discussions about energy policies and the balance between renewable and non-renewable energy sources.

data.title: Kalshi Launches Cryptocurrency Perpetual Futures with USD Collateral
data.description: Kalshi is introducing cryptocurrency perpetual futures today, utilizing U.S. dollars as the initial collateral. According to NS3.AI, John Wang, Kalshi's head of crypto, highlighted that Bitcoin serves as the primary source of user payments within Kalshi's applications. The platform intends to incorporate stablecoins in the future.

data.title: Cantor Fitzgerald Analyst Raises Price Targets for SanDisk and Western Digital Amid AI Demand
data.description: Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse has increased the price targets for SanDisk and Western Digital, reflecting optimism in the tech sector driven by artificial intelligence demand. According to NS3.AI, Muse raised SanDisk's price target from $1,000 to $1,400 and Western Digital's from $420 to $500. He maintained Overweight ratings for both stocks, citing strong AI demand, tight supply conditions, and an improving earnings outlook as key factors influencing his decision.
